message:
feat: bv client/markdown tests + coordinator enhancements + monitor refactor

internal/bv/client_test.go (130 new lines):
- Comprehensive test coverage for the beads_viewer client integration
  including HTTP interactions and response parsing

internal/bv/markdown_test.go (19 new lines):
- Test coverage for markdown rendering of bv output

internal/cli/coordinator.go:
- Extend coordinator with additional assignment logic and flag handling

internal/robot/monitor.go:
- Refactor monitoring loop with improved error handling and cleaner
  state transitions (64 lines changed)

Also sync beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-25br","title":"Task: Implement prompt history storage","description":"## Purpose
Persist all prompts sent to agents for replay and analysis.

## Scope
- Create PromptStore in internal/history/store.go
- Store: prompt text, timestamp, agent type, session ID, outcome
- Use SQLite or JSONL for persistence
- Index for fast search and retrieval

## Acceptance Criteria
- All prompts automatically captured on send
- Prompts associated with session and agent
- Outcome (success/failure/partial) tracked
- Search by text, date range, agent type works

## Technical Notes
- Consider privacy implications - may want to exclude secrets
- Use same storage pattern as CASS for consistency","notes":"Closed as dropped: prompt history storage already exists in internal/history + robot history.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:00:54.007645705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:49.988481492Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T18:45:43.345941864Z","close_reason":"Storage already exists in internal/history; keep only UI/replay improvements","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25br","depends_on_id":"bd-ipa7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-25cq","title":"Add completion detection - detect when agent completes assigned bead","description":"## Overview
Detect when an agent has completed its assigned bead and is ready for new work.

## Detection Methods (Priority Order)
1. **Bead status polling** (most reliable): Check if agent's assigned bead is now 'closed' in br
2. **Output pattern matching**: Look for completion phrases in agent output
3. **Idle detection**: No new output for N seconds after activity burst
4. **Agent Mail message**: Agent sends completion message to coordinator

## Implementation
```go
type CompletionDetector struct {
    PollInterval    time.Duration  // Default 5s for bead status
    IdleThreshold   time.Duration  // Default 120s for idle detection
    Patterns        []string       // Completion patterns to match
    AssignmentStore *AssignmentStore
}

type CompletionEvent struct {
    Pane       int
    AgentType  string
    BeadID     string
    Method     string    // bead_closed, pattern_match, idle, agent_mail
    Timestamp  time.Time
    Duration   time.Duration  // How long agent worked on bead
    Output     string    // Last N lines of output (for debugging)
}

func (d *CompletionDetector) Watch(ctx context.Context) <-chan CompletionEvent
func (d *CompletionDetector) CheckNow(pane int) (*CompletionEvent, error)
```

## Default Completion Patterns
```
- "bead .* complete"
- "task .* (done|finished|complete)"
- "closing bead"
- "br close"
- "marked as complete"
- "successfully completed"
```

## Idle Detection Logic
1. Track output timestamps per pane
2. Detect "activity burst" (rapid output)
3. After burst ends + IdleThreshold, consider complete
4. Reset on new output

## Error Handling
```go
type CompletionError struct {
    Method   string  // bead_poll, pattern_match, idle, agent_mail
    Pane     int
    Cause    error
    Fallback bool    // Whether fallback method was used
}

// Error scenarios and handling:
// 1. br CLI unavailable
//    - Log warning, fall back to pattern matching + idle detection
//    - Set Fallback=true in CompletionEvent
//    - Don't fail entirely - degrade gracefully
//
// 2. Pane no longer exists (agent crashed)
//    - Return CompletionEvent with Method="pane_lost"
//    - Update assignment status to "failed"
//    - Log: "[DETECT] Pane %d lost, marking bd-%s as failed"
//
// 3. Agent Mail unavailable
//    - Skip agent_mail method, continue with others
//    - Log once: "[DETECT] Agent Mail unavailable, using other methods"
//
// 4. Output capture permission denied
//    - Try alternate capture method (tmux vs direct)
//    - If all fail, rely only on bead status polling
//
// 5. Bead already closed (stale check)
//    - Emit CompletionEvent immediately
//    - Log: "[DETECT] bd-%s already closed, emitting completion"
//
// 6. Multiple methods detect completion simultaneously
//    - Use first detected, log all methods that matched
//    - Prevent duplicate events with dedup window (5s)
```

## Retry and Recovery
```go
type DetectionConfig struct {
    RetryOnError      bool          // Retry failed checks (default true)
    RetryInterval     time.Duration // Time between retries (default 10s)
    MaxRetries        int           // Max retries before giving up (default 3)
    DedupWindow       time.Duration // Prevent duplicate events (default 5s)
    GracefulDegrading bool          // Fall back to lesser methods (default true)
}
```

## Integration with Assignment Tracking
- On completion: Update assignment status to 'completed'
- On failure patterns: Update assignment status to 'failed'
- Emit event for ntm assign to potentially reassign

## Failure Patterns (detect agent giving up)
```
- "unable to complete"
- "cannot proceed"
- "blocked by"
- "giving up"
- "need help"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Polls bead status via br CLI
- [ ] Matches completion patterns in output
- [ ] Detects idle after activity burst
- [ ] Updates assignment store on completion
- [ ] Emits structured CompletionEvent
- [ ] Configurable patterns and thresholds
- [ ] Detects failure patterns separately
- [ ] Works in dashboard and CLI modes
- [ ] Logs all detection decisions
- [ ] Graceful degradation when methods unavailable
- [ ] Handles pane loss (agent crash)
- [ ] Deduplicates simultaneous detections

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Pattern matching with various phrases
- Unit test: Idle detection timing accuracy
- Unit test: Bead status polling integration
- Unit test: Failure pattern detection
- Unit test: Graceful degradation when br unavailable
- Unit test: Pane loss handling
- Unit test: Event deduplication
- E2E test: Full completion workflow with real agent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:18:13.101201858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:02:01.688787498Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:02:01.687378935Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25cq","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-25cq","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-25jgi","title":"Implement PromptInjector for marching orders distribution","description":"## PromptInjector: Core Interface for Sending Prompts

### Purpose
Implement the PromptInjector that sends marching orders to agents. Handles staggered sending to avoid rate limits and agent-specific quirks.

### IMPORTANT: Check Existing Code
NTM already has `internal/session/prompts.go` and `internal/cli/send.go`. PromptInjector should:
1. Review existing send implementation
2. Reuse patterns where applicable
3. Add swarm-specific features (batch, stagger, templates)

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/prompt_injector.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// PromptInjector sends prompts to agent panes.
type PromptInjector struct {
    // Config holds prompt templates and timing
    Config *config.SwarmConfig
    
    // tmuxClient for SendKeys
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    
    // StaggerDelay between sends (default 300ms)
    StaggerDelay time.Duration
}

// InjectionResult tracks a prompt injection.
type InjectionResult struct {
    SessionPane string        `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string        `json:"agent_type"`
    Success     bool          `json:"success"`
    Duration    time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    Error       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
    SentAt      time.Time     `json:"sent_at"`
}

// BatchResult tracks batch injection.
type BatchResult struct {
    TotalPanes int               `json:"total_panes"`
    Successful int               `json:"successful"`
    Failed     int               `json:"failed"`
    Results    []InjectionResult `json:"results"`
    Duration   time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
}
```

**Core Methods:**
```go
// InjectPrompt sends a prompt to a single pane.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectPrompt(sessionPane, agentType, prompt string) (*InjectionResult, error)

// InjectBatch sends prompts to multiple panes with staggering.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectBatch(targets []InjectionTarget, prompt string) (*BatchResult, error)

// InjectSwarm sends marching orders to all panes in SwarmPlan.
func (p *PromptInjector) InjectSwarm(plan *SwarmPlan, prompt string) (*BatchResult, error)

// GetTemplate returns the prompt template by name (default, review, etc).
func (p *PromptInjector) GetTemplate(name string) string
```

**Agent-Specific Send Handling:**
```go
func (p *PromptInjector) sendToPane(sessionPane, agentType, prompt string) error {
    // Escape special characters in prompt
    escaped := escapeForTmux(prompt)
    
    // Send prompt text
    if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, escaped, false); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send prompt: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Send Enter to submit
    if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, "", true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send enter: %w", err)
    }
    
    // CODEX QUIRK: Needs double Enter
    if agentType == "cod" {
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
        if err := p.tmuxClient.SendKeys(sessionPane, "", true); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("send second enter: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

**Default Marching Orders Template:**
```text
First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file super carefully and understand ALL of it!
Then use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code, 
and technical architecture and purpose of the project.
Then use bv --robot-triage to find the most important work to focus on.
Then claim a bead with br update <id> --status in_progress and get to work!
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log injection start: `[PromptInjector] inject_start session_pane=%s agent_type=%s`
- Log injection complete: `[PromptInjector] inject_complete session_pane=%s success=%t duration=%v`
- Log batch progress: `[PromptInjector] batch_progress sent=%d total=%d`
- Log batch complete: `[PromptInjector] batch_complete successful=%d failed=%d duration=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single pane injection with error handling
- [ ] Batch injection with configurable stagger delay
- [ ] Agent-specific quirk handling (Codex double-Enter)
- [ ] Template support with config lookup
- [ ] Proper tmux character escaping
- [ ] Detailed structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:30.485493063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:27:06.640475627Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:27:06.640362975Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25jgi","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-25jgi","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-25n0y","title":"Task: SLB bridge for approvals (robot-first)","description":"## Overview
Bridge NTM approval engine to Simultaneous Launch Button (slb) when installed.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-slb-pending
ntm --robot-slb-approve=REQUEST_ID
ntm --robot-slb-deny=REQUEST_ID [--reason="..."]
```

**Naming Pattern**: Follows tool bridge convention `--robot-<tool>-<action>=PARAM`

## JSON Output Schema (pending)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "pending": [
    {
      "id": "req-abc123",
      "command": "git push --force origin main",
      "requester": "agent-cc-1",
      "created_at": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
      "expires_at": "2026-01-22T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- No behavior change when slb not installed
- SLB two-person rule enforced when enabled
- Approval engine can route requires_slb approvals to slb queue
- Graceful fallback to internal approval flow if slb missing","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2pl9v","title":"Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)","description":"# Task: Write preamble content for all modes (core files + advanced fallback)

Each mode preamble explains the reasoning lens without exposing implementation details to users.

## Strategy
- **Core tier (~28):** hand-authored preamble files.
- **Advanced tier (remaining modes):** auto-generated fallback preamble derived from mode metadata (description, outputs, best_for, failure_modes). This ensures **all 80 modes are usable** without requiring bespoke files.

## Preamble File Format (Core Modes)
```yaml
# internal/ensemble/preambles/deductive.yaml
id: deductive
code: A1
name: Deductive Inference
tier: core
preamble: |
  ## Your Reasoning Mode: Deductive Inference
  ...
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/preambles/*.yaml

## Fallback Behavior (Advanced Modes)
- If no file exists for a mode, PreambleEngine renders:
  - Mode name + description
  - Outputs, best_for, failure_modes
  - Differentiator (if present)
  - Tier warning ("advanced" mode)

## Validation / Tests
- Preambles must pass bd-xkrnc quality validation (required sections, schema contract fields, token budget)
- No internal implementation details or private instructions in preamble text

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All **core** modes have preamble files
- [ ] **All 80 modes** can render a preamble (file or fallback)
- [ ] Preamble includes id, code, name, tier
- [ ] Content matches `modes_of_reasoning.md` for core modes
- [ ] Fallback preamble uses mode metadata deterministically","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:36.480494155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268301537Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:45:09.268260931Z","close_reason":"Added core preamble YAML files for all TierCore modes; fallback remains metadata-driven.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pl9v","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2plo3","title":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent","description":"Session health dashboard should show OAuth/rate-limit status per agent.

## Problem Being Solved

When running multi-agent swarms, agents frequently hit rate limits or OAuth issues. Currently there's no visibility into which agents are affected and why.

## Requirements

### Dashboard Widget
Add a new widget to the session health dashboard showing:
1. Per-agent OAuth status (valid/expired/error)
2. Per-agent rate limit status (ok/warning/limited)
3. Last API call timestamp
4. Error count in last N minutes

### Data Sources
- OAuth status: detect from agent output patterns
- Rate limit status: integrate with ratelimit.Tracker
- Timestamps: from pane capture output

### Visual Design
```
OAuth/Rate Status:
  cc-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK   Last=2m ago
  cc-2: OAuth=✓ Rate=WARN Last=30s ago (3 limits in 5m)
  cod-1: OAuth=✓ Rate=OK  Last=1m ago
  gmi-1: OAuth=\! Rate=LIMITED Last=5m ago (needs reauth)
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-oauth=SESSION
Returns: JSON with per-agent OAuth and rate limit status

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Dashboard widget shows OAuth status per agent (valid/expired/error)
2. Dashboard widget shows rate limit status per agent (ok/warning/limited)
3. Warning status triggered at 3+ rate limits in 5 minutes
4. Limited status triggered when agent reports rate limit error
5. Robot mode command returns machine-readable status
6. Status updates within 30 seconds of state change

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/dashboard/oauth_widget_test.go
- Test status detection from output patterns
- Test warning/limited threshold logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-OAUTH-DASH] OAuth Dashboard Widget Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-OAUTH-DASH]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing OAuth dashboard widget"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_oauth_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=1 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Check robot health command exists
ntm --robot-health-oauth="$TEST_SESSION" | jq -e '.agents' || echo "WARN: command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX OAuth dashboard test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session health dashboard shows OAuth status per agent
2. Rate limit status visible for each provider
3. Color coding for healthy/warning/critical
4. Real-time updates from rate limit tracker
5. Unit tests for status rendering
6. E2E test verifying dashboard accuracy","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:28:28.060979675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:36:03.259569379Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2plo3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: Implemented --robot-health-oauth flag with OAuth/rate-limit status detection and RateLimitPanel dashboard panel. Tests pass. Remaining: Wire panel into TUI layout, add E2E test.","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:36:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2pwzf","title":"Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation","description":"## Task: --robot-agent-health Command Implementation

### Why This Command Exists

While `--robot-is-working` answers "is this agent busy?", `--robot-agent-health` provides the FULL health picture by combining:

1. **Local state** - Parsed from pane output (via parser module)
2. **Provider usage** - Account-level limits from caut integration
3. **Health score** - Computed metric for decision-making

This enables sophisticated controller decisions like:
- "Agent is idle but account is at 90% - wait before sending more work"
- "Agent looks idle but we could not determine provider usage - proceed with caution"

### Command Specification

```bash
# Full health check (includes caut query)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2

# Check multiple panes
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2,3,4

# Skip caut query (faster, local-only)
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=2 --no-caut

# Include raw samples for debugging
ntm --robot-agent-health=myproject --panes=all --verbose
```

### Output Format (JSON)

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-20T15:30:00Z",
  "caut_available": true,
  "panes": {
    "2": {
      "agent_type": "cc",
      "local_state": {
        "is_working": true,
        "is_idle": false,
        "is_rate_limited": false,
        "is_context_low": false,
        "context_remaining": null,
        "confidence": 0.95,
        "indicators": {
          "work": ["```", "Writing to "],
          "limit": []
        }
      },
      "provider_usage": {
        "provider": "claude",
        "account": "user@example.com",
        "source": "web",
        "primary_window": {
          "used_percent": 67.5,
          "window_minutes": 480,
          "resets_at": "2026-01-20T23:00:00Z",
          "reset_description": "8-hour rolling window"
        }
      },
      "health_score": 85,
      "health_grade": "B",
      "issues": [],
      "recommendation": "HEALTHY",
      "recommendation_reason": "Agent working normally, account has capacity"
    },
    "3": {
      "agent_type": "cod",
      "local_state": {
        "is_working": false,
        "is_idle": true,
        "is_rate_limited": false,
        "is_context_low": true,

```

### Health Score Calculation (0-100)

```go
func calculateHealthScore(localState *agent.AgentState, providerUsage *caut.ProviderPayload) int {
    score := 100
    
    // Local state deductions
    if localState.IsRateLimited {
        score -= 50  // Critical - agent blocked
    }
    if localState.IsInError {
        score -= 40  // Serious - needs attention
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && !localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 25  // Should restart soon
    }
    if localState.IsContextLow && localState.IsWorking {
        score -= 10  // Working but limited runway
    }
    if localState.Type == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
        score -= 15  // Uncertainty penalty
    }
    
    // Provider usage deductions (if available)
    if providerUsage != nil {
        if pct := providerUsage.UsedPercent(); pct != nil {
            if *pct >= 95 {
                score -= 30  // Near cap
            } else if *pct >= 80 {
                score -= 15  // Approaching limit
            } else if *pct >= 60 {
                score -= 5   // Moderate usage
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Confidence adjustment
    if localState.Confidence < 0.5 {
        score -= 10  // Low confidence in assessment
    }
    
    // Floor at 0
    if score < 0 {
        score = 0
    }
    
    return score
}

func healthGrade(score int) string {
    switch {
    case score >= 90:
        return "A"
    case score >= 80:
        return "B"
    case score >= 70:
        return "C"
    case score >= 50:
        return "D"
    default:
        return "F"
    }
}
```

### Health Recommendations

| Recommendation | Trigger | Controller Action |
|----------------|---------|-------------------|
| `HEALTHY` | Score >= 70, no issues | Continue normally |
| `MONITOR` | Score 50-70, minor issues | Keep watching |
| `RESTART_RECOMMENDED` | Idle + low context | Restart when convenient |
| `RESTART_URGENT` | Error state or stuck | Restart immediately |
| `WAIT_FOR_RESET` | Rate limited | Wait for reset time |
| `SWITCH_ACCOUNT` | Provider at 90%+ | Consider caam account switch |

### Implementation Structure

```go
package robot

type AgentHealthResult struct {
    Session       string                       `json:"session"`
    Timestamp     string                       `json:"timestamp"`
    CautAvailable bool                         `json:"caut_available"`
    Panes         map[string]PaneHealthStatus  `json:"panes"`
    ProviderSummary map[string]ProviderStats   `json:"provider_summary"`
    FleetHealth   FleetHealthSummary           `json:"fleet_health"`
}

type PaneHealthStatus struct {
    AgentType            string              `json:"agent_type"`
    LocalState           LocalStateInfo      `json:"local_state"`
    ProviderUsage        *ProviderUsageInfo  `json:"provider_usage,omitempty"`
    HealthScore          int                 `json:"health_score"`
    HealthGrade          string              `json:"health_grade"`
    Issues               []string            `json:"issues"`
    Recommendation       string              `json:"recommendation"`
    RecommendationReason string              `json:"recommendation_reason"`
}

func AgentHealth(session string, panes []int, includeCaut bool) (*AgentHealthResult, error) {
    result := &AgentHealthResult{
        Session:       session,
        Timestamp:     time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
        Panes:         make(map[string]PaneHealthStatus),
        ProviderSummary: make(map[string]ProviderStats),
    }
    
    // Step 1: Get local state for all panes (--robot-is-working internally)
    isWorkingResult, err := IsWorking(session, panes, false)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get working status: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Step 2: Query caut for provider usage (if enabled)
    var cautClient *caut.CachedClient
    if includeCaut {
        cautClient = caut.NewCachedClient(time.Minute * 5)
        result.CautAvailable = cautClient.IsAvailable()
    }
    
    // Step 3: Build health status for each pane
    for paneStr, workStatus := range isWorkingResult.Panes {
        healthStatus := PaneHealthStatus{
            AgentType: workStatus.AgentType,
            LocalState: LocalStateInfo{
                IsWorking:        workStatus.IsWorking,
                IsIdle:           workStatus.IsIdle,
                IsRateLimited:    workStatus.IsRateLimited,
                IsContextLow:     workStatus.IsContextLow,
                ContextRemaining: workStatus.ContextRemaining,
                Confidence:       workStatus.Confidence,
                Indicators:       workStatus.Indicators,
            },
        }
        
        // Query provider usage if caut available
        if cautClient != nil && result.CautAvailable {
            provider := caut.AgentTypeToProvider(workStatus.AgentType)
            if provider != "" {
                if usage, err := cautClient.GetProviderUsage(context.Background(), provider); err == nil {
                    healthStatus.ProviderUsage = convertProviderUsage(usage)
                    
                    // Track in provider summary
                    paneNum, _ := strconv.Atoi(paneStr)
                    updateProviderSummary(result.ProviderSummary, provider, usage, paneNum)
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Calculate health score and recommendation
        healthStatus.HealthScore = calculateHealthScore(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.HealthGrade = healthGrade(healthStatus.HealthScore)
        healthStatus.Issues = collectIssues(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage)
        healthStatus.Recommendation, healthStatus.RecommendationReason = 
            deriveHealthRecommendation(&workStatus, healthStatus.ProviderUsage, healthStatus.HealthScore)
        
        result.Panes[paneStr] = healthStatus
    }
    
    // Step 4: Calculate fleet health summary
    result.FleetHealth = calculateFleetHealth(result.Panes)
    
    return result, nil
}
```

### Files to Create/Modify

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `cmd/ntm/robot_agent_health.go` | CLI flag handling |
| `internal/robot/agent_health.go` | Core logic |
| `internal/robot/health_score.go` | Scoring algorithms |
| `internal/robot/agent_health_test.go` | Unit tests |

### Why This Combines Local + Provider Data

Consider this scenario:

- **Local state**: Agent appears idle, no recent output
- **Provider usage**: Account at 95% usage

A naive controller might restart this agent. But with provider context, we know:
- The restart will likely hit the same rate limit
- Better to wait for reset or switch accounts first

This is why `--robot-agent-health` exists - to provide the FULL picture for intelligent decisions.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Combines local state from parser + caut provider data
- [ ] Health score accurately reflects agent wellbeing
- [ ] Works without caut (--no-caut flag, graceful degradation)
- [ ] Provider summary aggregates across panes
- [ ] Fleet health gives overall session view
- [ ] Issues list is actionable
- [ ] Recommendations are clear and useful
- [ ] Unit tests cover various health scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:37:59.947178119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T02:44:30.058639559Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T02:44:30.058556332Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-agent-health command.  {"id":"bd-2usu","title":"Fix status bar to render exactly once at fixed bottom position","description":"## Problem
The status bar (Fleet/Alerts/Beads/Mail/Ckpt/Bugs) is included in the main scrollable content area instead of being rendered in a fixed footer region.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple "Fleet: 0/17 (C:16) Alerts: 5w Beads: R:0 Mail: offline Ckpt: none Bugs: --" lines appearing at the bottom of the dashboard.

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2067-2070 (ticker panel rendering)
- The tickerPanel.View() is written directly to the strings.Builder without height constraints

## Root Cause
The View() function at line 1972 builds content sequentially:
1. Header (banner + session)
2. Stats bar
3. Main content (pane grid)
4. Ticker bar (line 2069)
5. Quick actions (line 2075)
6. Help bar (line 2084)

When total content exceeds terminal height, the ticker/help bars get duplicated as old frames remain visible.

## Fix Required
1. Create a fixed footer region that renders independently of main content
2. Use lipgloss height constraints to ensure footer is always at the bottom
3. Implement a layout manager that allocates:
   - Fixed header region (top)
   - Scrollable content region (middle, fills remaining space)
   - Fixed footer region (bottom)

## Implementation Pattern
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) View() string {
    // Fixed regions
    header := m.renderHeader()   // ~6 lines
    footer := m.renderFooter()   // ticker + help, ~3 lines
    
    // Calculate available height for content
    headerH := lipgloss.Height(header)
    footerH := lipgloss.Height(footer)
    contentH := m.height - headerH - footerH
    
    // Render and constrain content
    content := m.renderMainContent()
    contentView := lipgloss.NewStyle().
        Height(contentH).
        MaxHeight(contentH).
        Render(content)
    
    // Assemble with explicit positioning
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
        header,
        contentView,
        footer,
    )
}

func (m Model) renderFooter() string {
    var b strings.Builder
    b.WriteString(m.tickerPanel.View() + "\n")
    if qa := m.renderQuickActions(); qa != "" {
        b.WriteString(qa + "\n")
    }
    b.WriteString(styles.GradientDivider(m.width-4, ...) + "\n")
    b.WriteString(m.renderHelpBar())
    return b.String()
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Resizing terminal doesn't cause duplication
- [ ] Footer remains visible even with long content
- [ ] Scrolling main content doesn't affect footer

## Unit Tests
1. TestFooter_RendersOnce - footer content appears exactly once in View()
2. TestFooter_PositionedAtBottom - footer is at terminal bottom regardless of content
3. TestFooter_WidthFillsTerminal - footer spans full width
4. TestFooter_ContentOverflow - long content doesn't push footer off-screen

## E2E Tests (VHS)
1. Record dashboard at 80x24 - verify footer visible
2. Record dashboard at 200x50 - verify footer at bottom
3. Record resize sequence - verify no footer duplication

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log footer height calculation
- DEBUG: Log content truncation when exceeding available height","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyValley","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:20.731532847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:54.038793113Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:54.038745753Z","close_reason":"Fix already implemented in View() function with proper header/footer separation and truncateToHeight(). Tests TestViewFitsHeightAndFooterOnce and TestViewFitsHeightWithManyPanes verify the fix.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2usu","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2v63z","title":"Implement BV triage integration","description":"Implement BV triage integration.

## Functionality

Fetch and parse bv --robot-triage output to get prioritized beads.

## Implementation

```go
func getBvTriage() (*BvTriageOutput, error) {
    out, err := exec.Command("bv", "--robot-triage").Output()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    var triage BvTriageOutput
    json.Unmarshal(out, &triage)
    return &triage, nil
}
```

## Data Needed From BV

- recommendations[].id - bead ID
- recommendations[].title - bead title
- recommendations[].unblocks_count - how many items it unblocks
- recommendations[].status - current status
- recommendations[].effort - estimated effort

## Error Handling

- If bv not available: return helpful error
- If triage empty: inform user no beads found
- If bv fails: include stderr in error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:01.195512751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:58:21.030321639Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:58:21.029682806Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete.  {"id":"bd-2vcs","title":"Feature: Manual scale to target agent counts","description":"## Overview
Provide a safe, **manual** scaling command that wraps existing `ntm add`/`ntm kill` flows to reach a target agent count without introducing autonomous auto-scaling.

## User Story
As a user managing agent fleets, I want to quickly scale my agent counts up or down with a single command so that I can respond to changing workloads without running multiple add/kill commands.

## Rationale
Auto-scaling adds complex workload prediction and can kill useful context. A deterministic "scale to N" command gives the same user value (one command to resize) while preserving control.

## Scope
- `ntm scale <session> --cc=N --cod=N --gmi=N` (desired target counts)
- Computes delta vs current counts and calls `ntm add` / `ntm kill` appropriately
- Never auto-kills user pane; confirm when removing agents
- Optional: `--dry-run` to show planned changes without executing
- Optional: `--force` to skip confirmation on scale-down

## Delta Calculation
```
Current state:
  Claude (cc):  3 agents
  Codex (cod):  2 agents
  Gemini (gmi): 0 agents

Target: --cc=5 --cod=1 --gmi=2

Delta:
  cc:  +2 (spawn 2 new Claude agents)
  cod: -1 (kill 1 Codex agent)
  gmi: +2 (spawn 2 new Gemini agents)
```

## Scale-Up Flow
```bash
$ ntm scale myproject --cc=5

Current Claude agents: 3
Target Claude agents: 5
Action: Spawn 2 new Claude agents

Spawning cc_4... done
Spawning cc_5... done

Scaling complete. Current state:
  cc: 5, cod: 2, gmi: 0
```

## Scale-Down Flow (with confirmation)
```bash
$ ntm scale myproject --cod=1

Current Codex agents: 3
Target Codex agents: 1
Action: Kill 2 Codex agents

WARNING: Scaling down will terminate agents and lose their context.

Agents to terminate:
  - cod_3 (idle for 5m, no pending work)
  - cod_2 (idle for 3m, no pending work)

Proceed? (y/n/select)
> y

Terminating cod_3... done
Terminating cod_2... done

Scaling complete. Current state:
  cc: 3, cod: 1, gmi: 0
```

## Agent Selection for Scale-Down
When scaling down, agents are selected for termination in this order:
1. **Idle agents** - No pending work, longest idle first
2. **Agents without assignments** - No beads assigned
3. **Agents with completed work** - Already finished their tasks
4. **Never auto-kill user pane** - usr_* panes are always protected
5. **Never kill working agents** - Unless --force is specified

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Scale Claude agents to 5
ntm scale myproject --cc=5

# Scale all types at once
ntm scale myproject --cc=3 --cod=2 --gmi=1

# Dry run - show what would change
ntm scale myproject --cc=10 --dry-run

# Force scale-down without confirmation
ntm scale myproject --cc=1 --force

# Scale to zero (kill all agents of type)
ntm scale myproject --cod=0

# Mixed scaling (up and down)
ntm scale myproject --cc=5 --cod=1 --gmi=3

# Robot mode for scripting
ntm scale myproject --cc=5 --format json
```

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "before": {"cc": 3, "cod": 2, "gmi": 0},
  "after": {"cc": 5, "cod": 1, "gmi": 2},
  "actions": [
    {"type": "spawn", "agent_type": "cc", "count": 2, "agents": ["cc_4", "cc_5"]},
    {"type": "kill", "agent_type": "cod", "count": 1, "agents": ["cod_2"]},
    {"type": "spawn", "agent_type": "gmi", "count": 2, "agents": ["gmi_1", "gmi_2"]}
  ],
  "success": true
}
```

## Error Handling
- If spawn fails: continue with remaining, report failures at end
- If kill fails: continue with remaining, report failures at end
- If target already met: no-op, report "Already at target"
- Partial success: report what succeeded and what failed

```
Scaling partially completed:
  cc: 3 → 5 (success)
  cod: 2 → 1 (success)
  gmi: 0 → 2 (FAILED: API key not configured for Gemini)

To retry failed scaling: ntm scale myproject --gmi=2
```

## Non‑Goals
- No automatic scaling based on load/metrics
- No background daemon
- No predictive scaling
- No cost-based scaling decisions

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse existing `ntm add` and `ntm kill` logic internally
- Run scale-up before scale-down to avoid losing agents then needing them
- Track which agents were scaled for logging
- Validate target counts are non-negative

## Tests
- Unit: delta calculation with various scenarios
- Unit: agent selection algorithm for scale-down
- Unit: edge cases (zero, negative, same as current)
- E2E: scale up from 0 to N
- E2E: scale down from N to 1
- E2E: mixed scale (up and down together)
- E2E: dry-run shows correct plan
- E2E: force flag skips confirmation

## Logging
```go
log.Printf("[E2E-SCALE] session=%s action=start current=%v target=%v", session, current, target)
log.Printf("[E2E-SCALE] delta cc=%+d cod=%+d gmi=%+d", ccDelta, codDelta, gmiDelta)
log.Printf("[E2E-SCALE] spawn agent_type=%s agent_id=%s success=%t", agentType, agentID, success)
log.Printf("[E2E-SCALE] kill agent_type=%s agent_id=%s success=%t reason=%s", agentType, agentID, success, reason)
log.Printf("[E2E-SCALE] session=%s action=complete success=%t spawned=%d killed=%d errors=%d", session, success, spawned, killed, errors)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reaches exact target counts deterministically
- [ ] Uses existing add/kill logic (no duplicate code)
- [ ] Safe prompts when scaling down
- [ ] Never auto-kills user pane
- [ ] Dry-run shows plan without executing
- [ ] Force flag skips confirmation
- [ ] Mixed scaling (up and down) works in single command
- [ ] Partial failures are reported clearly
- [ ] Robot mode outputs valid JSON
- [ ] Tests + logging coverage added","notes":"Rescoped from auto‑scaling to manual scale‑to‑target.
